stories.title: Pakistan hosts historic face-to-face talks between the US and Iran
stories.description: In Pakistan's capital, Islamabad, officials from the US and Iran met to discuss a possible end to the war that's engulfed much of the Middle East.

stories.title: What's it like to cover the Ukraine war as the world's attention is focused on Iran?
stories.description: In this week's Reporter's Notebook conversation, we take a closer look at what it's like to cover the war in Ukraine, and how the war in Iran is changing that.

stories.title: Pope Leo says 'delusion of omnipotence' is fueling U.S.-Israeli war in Iran
stories.description: In the first weeks of the war, the Chicago-born Leo was initially reluctant to publicly condemn the violence and limited his comments to muted appeals for peace and dialogue. But Leo stepped up his criticism starting on Palm Sunday.

stories.title: U.S.-Iran peace talks underway in Islamabad after weeks of frantic diplomacy
stories.description: A senior White House official said that an American delegation, led by Vice President JD Vance, was meeting with senior Iranian negotiators in Islamabad to discuss an end to the six-week long war.

stories.title: Historic Vance-Ghalibaf talks must bridge deep distrust
stories.description: Face-to-face talks would mark the highest-level engagement between Iran and the US since the 1979 Islamic Revolution.

stories.title: Trump says Iran's handling of Strait of Hormuz is 'not the agreement we have'
stories.description: The US president accused Iran of "doing a very poor job" with the waterway as Israel and Lebanon to begin peace talks.

stories.title: 'Endless fears': Even if fighting stops, the damage to Iran's children will endure
stories.description: The BBC has been able to obtain testimony from parents and those trying to help children deal with the distress that comes with war.

stories.title: Lebanon thought there was a ceasefire - then Israel unleashed deadly blitz
stories.description: Israel says Lebanon is not included in the ceasefire the US agreed to halt the war with Iran.

stories.title: Has US achieved its war objectives in Iran?
stories.description: Key US objectives at the start of the war were to stop Iran getting a nuclear weapon and degrading its arsenal.

stories.title: Israeli strikes in Lebanon 'grave violation' of ceasefire, Iran minister tells BBC
stories.description: Iran's deputy foreign minister says the US must choose between 'war and ceasefire'.

stories.title: How many ships are crossing the Strait of Hormuz?
stories.description: Only a few vessels have crossed the strait since the US-Iran ceasefire deal, according to BBC Verify analysis.

stories.title: Negotiators face huge task to close gaps in rival Iran peace proposals
stories.description: The US has a 15-point plan to end the war, with Iran offering 10 proposals of its own. Can they be reconciled?

stories.title: At least 182 killed across Lebanon in large wave of Israeli strikes
stories.description: Attacks hit the southern suburbs of Beirut, southern Lebanon and the eastern Bekaa Valley, hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced.

stories.title: Israel backs US-Iran ceasefire but Netanyahu's war goals remain unfulfilled
stories.description: The Israeli prime minister's response to the ceasefire has been muted, in contrast to the triumphal statements from the US and Iran.

stories.title: Why ceasefire deal with US has unsettled Iran's hardliners
stories.description: The two-week truce opens up the prospect of direct talks with the US but has angered Iran's hardliners, writes BBC News Persian’s Kasra Naji.

stories.title: Iran ceasefire deal gives Trump a way out of war - but at a high cost
stories.description: The path to the two-week ceasefire with Iran may have fundamentally altered the way the rest of the world views the US.

stories.title: What we know about the two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.description: The provisional truce comes more than a month after the US and Israel launched coordinated attacks on Iran.

stories.title: How Pakistan helped secure a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran
stories.description: Pakistan has a historic relationship with Iran, but an agreement was far from certain, writes the BBC’s Caroline Davies

stories.title: Little sign of breakthrough as Trump's Iran deadline nears
stories.description: The US president is in a delicate political position as the final hours to Tuesday's deadline for Iran to open the Strait of Hormuz tick down.

stories.title: Iran-US negotiations underway in Pakistan, US says Strait of Hormuz being 'cleared'
stories.description: Delegations from the United States and Iran held direct negotiations on Saturday for the first time since 1979 in Islamabad,Pakistan, to end a six-week conflict, while US President Donald Trump said that the US military had begun clearing mines from the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: Iran: a mediator on the mediations
stories.description: Gavin Lee speaks with Oliver McTernan, Director of Forward Thinking, about the current situation in Iran, the Straight of Hormuz and Pakistan.

stories.title: 'The Last Cedar of Lebanon': A play exploring war’s lasting scars
stories.description: Aïda Asgharzadeh was widely praised for her powerful exploration of dissidence and exile in Persian Dolls, weaving her own family’s story into a drama spanning Iran and France. Her deft use of history as a backdrop to intimate, personal narratives is once again in focus with her ...

stories.title: Israel army says hit over 200 Hezbollah targets in last 24 hours
stories.description: Clouds of smoke were once again seen over villages in southern Lebanon after Israeli strikes targeted areas near the border. The attacks come as Iran and the United States begin peace talks, but Tehran has said it will not engage in negotiations until Israel halts its bombing cam...

stories.title: Iran, US top officials meet face-to-face
stories.description: The United States and Iran began negotiations Saturday in Pakistan, days after a fragile two-week ceasefire was announced, as the war that has killed thousands of people and shaken global markets entered its seventh week. FRANCE 24's Andrew Hilliar tells us more.

stories.title: Mexico seeks to curb reliance on US gas
stories.description: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum has announced plans to use new hydraulic fracturing technologies, or fracking, to reduce Mexico’s reliance on foreign energy amid the Iran war. She framed the approach as a form of sustainable extraction, but communities who say they have been ...

stories.title: Who is Iran's Chief negotiator Ghalibaf?
stories.description: The 70-member Iranian delegation taking part in negotiations in Pakistan today is being led by one of Tehran’s most prominent non-clerical figures, Iranian Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf. Caroline Baum takes a look at the man Iran has entrusted with securing an end t...

stories.title: US, Iran sit down for talks in Islamabad
stories.description: Pakistani Prime Minister Shehbaz Sharif and US Vice President JD Vance met in Islamabad on Saturday as peace talks to end the broadening war in the Middle East got under way. The US delegation is set to meet Iranian officials later in the day. The latest updates with our France 2...

stories.title: Israel refuses ceasefire with Hezbollah
stories.description: Israel and Lebanon are due to hold talks in Washington on Tuesday, though a ceasefire remains a distant prospect. Israel says it will not discuss any halt to the fighting with Hezbollah, which it considers the main obstacle to peace. The meeting will mark the first formal contact...

stories.title: Iran war live: Historic face-to-face talks with US continue in Islamabad
stories.description: Iran and US continue direct talks in Islamabad, with the Strait of Hormuz believed to be a key sticking point.

stories.title: US President Trump says US ‘wins’ regardless of how Iran talks go
stories.description: US President Donald Trump says ‘we win regardless of what happens’ in Iran talks.

stories.title: “Diplomacy is not an event, it’s a process, it takes time.”
stories.description: Former Pakistani diplomat says expectations from the Islamabad talks between the US and Iran should be realistic.

stories.title: Has Israeli society become conditioned to permanent war?
stories.description: Opinion polls indicate opposition to ending war against Iran.

stories.title: Iran’s deputy FM says Tehran has ‘upper hand’ in talks with US
stories.description: Iran’s Gharibabadi says Tehran has entered negotiations from a position of strength and the war on Iran has failed.

stories.title: US says two naval ships ‘transited’ Strait of Hormuz for mine-clearing
stories.description: Negotiations are ongoing between US-Iranian delegations in Islamabad, with control of the strait a key sticking point.

stories.title: Pakistan sends fighter jets to Saudi Arabia amid fragile US-Iran ceasefire
stories.description: Deployment under mutual defence pact comes as Islamabad hosts US-Iran ceasefire talks.

stories.title: Iranian rabbi describes Israel’s destruction of a Tehran synagogue
stories.description: Iranian rabbi describes how he was informed that one of Tehran’s few synagogues had been destroyed in an Israel strike.

stories.title: Ceasefire brings some relief for Iranians but economic outlook remains grim
stories.description: Activity picks up in Tehran's Grand Bazaar compared with before the ceasefire, but vendors say sales remain slow.

stories.title: Strait of Hormuz leverage looms over US-Iran talks in Islamabad
stories.description: US and Iran hold first direct talks since 1979 in Islamabad, with progress hinging on the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: US warships transit Strait of Hormuz in mine clearance operation: Centcom
stories.description: Two US Navy warships transited the Strait of Hormuz at the start of an operation to clear the strategic waterway of mines laid by Iran, US Central Command (Centcom) said on Saturday. The announcement — which marks the first such transit since the US-Israeli war on Iran began — ca...

stories.title: ‘15 hours and counting’, says White House official as Iran, US delegations search for breakthrough
stories.description: The direct talks between delegations from Iran and the United States, mediated by Pakistan, stretched into a second day on Sunday, with a White House official being quoted by the US media as saying that the negotiations were ongoing for “15 hours and counting”. Meanwhile, the Ira...

stories.title: Pakistan sends military force, fighter jets to Saudi Arabia
stories.description: Pakistan has sent a military force and fighter jets to Saudi Arabia to boost security under a joint defence cooperation agreement between the two countries, the Saudi defence ministry said on Saturday. The Saudi ministry, in a statement, wrote that a military force and fighter je...

stories.title: Relatives seek FO’s help to ascertain fate of family members believed to be killed by missile debris in Kuwait
stories.description: TOBA TEK SINGH: Relatives of a Pakistan-origin man residing in Kuwait, who has been missing along with his family, have sought the authorities’ assistance to confirm whether the family had been killed in an incident involving missile debris after interception. Muhammad Saleem, a ...

stories.title: Easter truce between Russia, Ukraine begins
stories.description: A temporary truce between Russia and Ukraine entered into force on Saturday, with Kyiv warning it would respond “immediately” if Russia violated it. Russian President Vladimir Putin ordered the ceasefire on Thursday to coincide with Orthodox Easter, more than a week after Ukraini...

stories.title: US President Trump projects cautious optimism ahead of Iran talks
stories.description: WASHINGTON: As indirect diplomacy between the United States and Iran converges on Islamabad in the coming hours, the messaging from Washington has settled into a carefully managed tone of cautious optimism—tempered by clear acknowledgment that the process remains fragile. US Pres...

stories.title: Israeli strike kills 13 security personnel in Lebanon
stories.description: • Ceasefire talks loom as Lebanese death toll in war nears 1,900• Air raid sirens wail across northern Israel amid fresh rocket barrage• UN warns of looming food crisis as conflict sends prices soaring BEIRUT: An Israeli stri­ke on the southern Lebanese city of Nabatiyeh killed a...

stories.title: Conscientious objections to war rise sharply in US
stories.description: • Growing number of service members seeking information on how to apply for discharge• March alone saw over 80 new cases, nearly double annual norm• School bombing in Iran cited as key turning point by many callers WASHINGTON: The US-Israeli war against Iran has caused an unprece...

stories.title: Starmer says Nato in America’s ‘interests’ as Gulf tour ends
stories.description: BRITISH PM Keir Starmer shakes hands with his Qatari counterpart Mohammed bin Abdulrahman.—Reuters • Calls alliance a ‘defensive’ bloc that has ensured decades of safety for Europe• Comments follow Trump’s threats to withdraw after allies refuse to join Iran war• PM says Britain ...

stories.title: War impact on fertilisers worries food producers
stories.description: PARIS: Even as Gulf tanker traffic slowly resumes, the road back to normal food production will be long and arduous, given the war’s impact on fertiliser supplies, the UN has warned. With factories shuttered and soaring gas prices driving up production costs around the world, fer...

stories.title: Russia, Ukraine agree on truce for Orthodox Easter
stories.description: KYIV: Russia and Ukraine have agreed to a ceasefire over Orthodox Easter, a temporary truce that the Kremlin says will begin on Saturday afternoon and end on Sunday night. Russian President Vladimir Putin ordered the ceasefire late on Thursday, more than a week after Ukrainian Pr...

stories.title: Islamabad keyed up for ‘make-or-break’ talks
stories.description: • US-Iran dialogue set to begin after last-minute Lebanon hurdle eased• Backchannel diplomacy led by Pakistan helps secure partial pause in strikes• Iranian delegation arrives in Islamabad; Vance-led US team was due early morning• Tehran conditioned participation on ceasefire in ...

stories.title: Tears of joy mark reopening of Jerusalem holy sites after 41 days
stories.description: • Worshippers describe an ‘indescribable happiness’, one calls it ‘a miracle’• Tensions flare as police disperse Muslims at Al Aqsa for Jewish visitors JERUSALEM : Just before 5am at Jerusalem’s Al Aqsa Mosque compound, a Muslim worshipper prayed in tears. A few hours later, simi...

stories.title: Israel blocks Spain from US-led centre monitoring Gaza truce
stories.description: Israel said on Friday it had blocked Spain from participating in the work of a US-led centre established to help stabilise post-war Gaza following the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas. The Civil-Military Coordination Centre (CMCC) in Kiryat Gat was set up after the ceasefire to...

stories.title: ‘World doesn’t have a plan B’: Bilawal stresses need for US-Iran talks to lead to peaceful resolution of conflict
stories.description: Former foreign minister and PPP Chairman Bilawal Bhutto Zardari has said that the world does not have a “plan B” if the US-Iran talks fail to yield the desired results and emphasised the need for the negotiations to lead to a peaceful resolution to the Middle East war. Bilawal sh...

stories.title: Israel-Iran war LIVE updates: Iran talks pause after 15-hour negotiation, disagreements remain
stories.description: Iran denies an earlier claim by the U.S. that two Navy destroyers transited the Strait Of Hormuz

stories.title: U.S., Iran talks extend into second day as strait showdown deepens
stories.description: In a break with precedent, Iranian and U.S. officials were meeting directly, alongside Pakistan, and not speaking through mediators who shuttled between rooms

stories.title: How was the U.S.-Iran ceasefire deal reached?
stories.description: How did the two-week ceasefire come about and how did Pakistan become involved in mediation? Why has Israel reacted negatively to the U.S.-Iran ceasefire negotiations? What is happening in Islamabad now, and what are the chances of the talks succeeding?

stories.title: U.S. warships transit Strait of Hormuz in mine clearance operation
stories.description: Iran "strongly rejected" Washington's claims that U.S. vessels entered the strait, military spokesperson Ebrahim Zolfaghari told state TV

stories.title: Iran’s uranium enrichment secures independence, peaceful atom — supreme leader
stories.description: Ali Khamenei added that the US and other countries are not interested in Iran's progress and have put forward conditions presupposing a complete halt of uranium enrichment on Iranian territory

stories.title: Tehran could weigh nuclear deal with US with Iran-based enrichment consortium — Axios
stories.description: According to the report, a sixth round of talks on the Iranian nuclear program between the US president’s Special Envoy Steve Witkoff and Iran’s top diplomat, Abbas Araghchi, could be held this weekend in a Middle Eastern country

stories.title: DPRK slams U.S. for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang
stories.description: The Democratic People's Republic of Korea (DPRK) has slammed the United States for orchestrating efforts to tighten financial sanctions against Pyongyang.
